his true anxiety about the situation.

Revealed → Opposite meaning
Suppressed → Forced down

Q7 Not checked
General Aptitude • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

How may numbers can be made with digits 0, 7, 8 which are greater than ‘0’ and less than a million?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
Number of ways to form one digit number = 2
‘0’ cannot be treated as one digit number
Number of ways to form two digit number = 2 × 3 = 6
Number of ways to form three digit number = 2 × 3 × 3 = 18
Number of ways to form four digit number = 2 × 33 = 54
Number of ways to form five digit number = 2 × 34 = 162
Number of ways to foron six digit number = 2 × 35 = 486
Total number of ways = 2 + 6 + 18 + 54 + 162 + 486 = 728

Q9 Not checked
General Aptitude • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Given below are four pairs of words. Identify the one which is different from others.

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
Murky as well as Dusky – both refer to something which is dark or cheerless – similar in meaning;
Inter means ‘to place’ and exhume means to dig out - opposite in meaning.
Effete means lacking in vigour whereas fructuous means productive or fertile – opposite in meaning.
Benediction is an utterance of good wishes while execration refers to denouncement i.e. both are
opposite in meaning.

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)

Assume relative permittivity of oxide is 4 and the absolute permittivity of free space is 8.85 × 10–14 F/cm. If the flatband voltage is 0 V, then the
work function of the metal (in eV) is _________. (upto two decimal places)

NAT
Correct answer: 4.04
Solution

4.04 (3.95 - 4.15)

 
Q38 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the circuit shown in the figure below:

 
 
 
If the circuit was switched ON and previous state of the circuit is not known, then which of the following input combinations will not result in a reliable output?
Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
For the NOR gate:
When the input A = 1, the output P = 0 always. But when input A = 0, the NOR gate has to wait for
the output of the AND gate.
For the AND gate :
When the input is B = 0, the output Q = 1 always. But when input B = 1, the gate has to wait for the output of the NOR gate.
So, when A = 0 and B = 1, both the gates will wait for the other gate’s output. Hence, the output
level cannot be exactly determined without knowing the previous state.
Thus, when A = 0 and B = 1, the output becomes unreliable.

Q39 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the circuit based on a p-n-p transistor shown in the figure below:

 
The transistor is biased by using a constant D.C. supply VCC and a small signal input voltage (Vin) is applied at the base of the transistor with the help of coupling capacitor CC. Which one of the following circuits correctly represents the small signal equivalent circuit of the amplifier?
Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
The three figures are equivalent except in the first figure the resistance R1 should have been a parallel combination of R1 and R2 and in the second figure, the direction of dependent current source is not correct for the Vπ polarity. Hence, option (c) is correct.
